name: [Meal Prep] Telegram -> captures (capture + decide)

parameters.text: ={{ $('Build capture/decision file').item.json.isControl ? '\u2699\ufe0f Command `/' + $('Build capture/decision file').item.json.command + '` queued (`' + $('Build capture/decision file').item.json.id + '`). Processing shortly.' : ($('Build capture/decision file').item.json.isDecision ? '\u2705 Decision noted (`' + $('Build capture/decision file').item.json.id + '`). I\u2019ll apply it on the next run.' : '\u2705 Captured `' + $('Build capture/decision file').item.json.id + '` as ' + $('Build capture/decision file').item.json.command + '. I\u2019ll triage it on the next run.') }}

_setup_notes: UPDATED for the Telegram-control feature: one workflow now handles capture (/feature,/bug,...), approval replies, AND remote-control commands.
A message that STARTS with status/next/go/run/build/review/stop/enable/disable -> captures/commands/<id>.md (Dispatch-Commands.ps1 on the PC does the routing -- see docs/09-automation.md).
A message that STARTS with approve/park/reject/clarify -> captures/decisions/<id>.md (the reply gate). Unaffected by this change.
Everything else is unchanged -> captures/inbox/<id>.md (capture). Existing capture behavior is untouched.
Re-import this file in n8n to activate; fill the 4 REPLACE_WITH_* placeholders (Telegram credential id x3, your numeric Telegram user id, GitHub PAT with Contents read+write).
Apply-Decisions.ps1 (on the PC, via run-claude.ps1) reads captures/decisions/ and updates PROPOSALS + BUILD_QUEUE.
Dispatch-Commands.ps1 (on the PC, via the new Command Dispatcher scheduled task) reads captures/commands/ and routes to the matching phase runner.
Get your Telegram user id from @userinfobot. Prefer an n8n Header Auth credential over the inline PAT before going live.
